name: Redtrail v2.0 — Universal Agentic Security Scanner
description: Extend Redtrail from a web-only scanner into a fully autonomous general-purpose penetration testing agent with a team of specialist agents (SMB, AD, binary exploitation, web fuzzing, privesc, etc.) that are activated by a retro-feedback knowledge graph based on runtime discoveries. Redtrail must operate as a blind external attacker — no pre-loaded environment knowledge. Success criteria: 100% of hacker-lab flags (87/87).

userStories.title: Clone Shannon reference repository
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Shannon autonomous pentesting repository cloned into redtrail/reference/ so that implementation agents can reference its prompt engineering patterns, multi-agent architecture, and exploitation methodology.
acceptanceCriteria: Add redtrail/reference/ to .gitignore so cloned repos are not committed
Verify key reference files exist: prompts/recon.txt, prompts/exploit-injection.txt, prompts/shared/_rules.txt, src/ai/claude-executor.ts

userStories.title: Implement shell_exec tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want a shell_exec tool that can execute arbitrary shell commands inside the hacker-toolbox container so that I can use all installed security tools (nmap, impacket, hydra, sqlmap, etc.).
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/shell_exec.rs implementing the Tool trait from redtrail/src/tools/mod.rs
Input JSON: { 'command': string (required), 'timeout': number (optional, default 120 seconds), 'working_dir': string (optional) }
Output JSON: { 'stdout': string, 'stderr': string, 'exit_code': number, 'timed_out': boolean }

Support two execution modes controlled by REDTRAIL_EXEC_MODE env var: 'docker' (default, runs via 'docker exec hacker-toolbox <command>') and 'local' (runs command directly via tokio::process::Command)
Truncate stdout/stderr to 50KB max to avoid overwhelming LLM context

Tool description must list key available tools in the toolbox: nmap, hydra, sqlmap, impacket-*, smbclient, redis-cli, mysql, psql, pwntools, chisel, kubectl, tcpdump, dig, ffuf, john, hashcat, linpeas, python3
Register the tool in create_tools() in redtrail/src/main.rs
Write unit tests for command execution, timeout handling, and output truncation
Reference Shannon's bash tool pattern in reference/shannon/src/ai/claude-executor.ts for prompt design

userStories.title: Extend Target type for network targets
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want the Target type to support IP ranges and network-level targets (not just URLs) so that Redtrail can scan entire networks, not just web applications.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify redtrail/src/types.rs: change Target.base_url from String to Option<String>
Add Target.hosts: Vec<String> field for IP addresses and CIDR ranges
Add Target.exec_mode: ExecMode field (enum with Docker and Local variants)
Add Credential struct: { username: String, password: Option<String>, hash: Option<String>, service: String, host: String }

Add new VulnType variants: WeakCredentials, DefaultCredentials, AnonymousAccess, MissingAuthentication, SSRF, CSRF, SSTI, FileUpload, Deserialization, PathTraversal, BufferOverflow, FormatString, KerberosAttack, ContainerEscape, DockerSocketExposure, KubernetesExposure, SupplyChainCompromise, CiCdInjection, PromptInjection, ToolPoisoning, DNSZoneTransfer, SMBMisconfiguration, PassTheHash, GoldenTicket, DependencyConfusion, DataExfiltration, InsecureConfiguration, SensitiveDataExposure
Update all references to target.base_url throughout the codebase to handle Option<String> (grep for base_url in runner.rs, main.rs, tools/*, attacks/*)
Ensure existing web-only functionality still works when base_url is Some and hosts is empty

userStories.title: Update CLI for network targets
userStories.description: As a user, I want CLI flags to specify network targets (--hosts) and increase turn budgets so that Redtrail can be used for both web and network pentesting.
acceptanceCriteria: Add --hosts flag to CLI in redtrail/src/main.rs: accepts comma-separated IPs or CIDR ranges (e.g., '172.20.0.0/24,10.0.0.1')
Add --max-turns flag: increase default from 20 to 500 for complex engagements
Ensure at least one of --target or --hosts is provided (error if neither)

userStories.title: Implement KnowledgeBase retro-feedback system
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want an adaptive knowledge base that accumulates discovered intelligence (hosts, credentials, flags, failed attempts) during execution and feeds it back into my context so that I can make informed decisions, chain attacks, and determine which specialist agents to activate next.
acceptanceCriteria: KnowledgeBase fields: discovered_hosts (Vec<HostInfo> with ip, ports, services, os), credentials (Vec<Credential>), access_levels (Vec<AccessLevel> with host, user, privilege_level, method), attack_paths (Vec<AttackPath> describing successful chains), flags (Vec<String> for FLAG{...} values), failed_attempts (Vec<FailedAttempt> with tool, target, description, timestamp), notes (Vec<String>), activated_specialists (Vec<String> tracking which specialists have run and on what findings)
Add KnowledgeBase to AgentState in redtrail/src/agent/mod.rs

Implement auto-extraction: after every tool execution in runner.rs, scan output for patterns — FLAG{...} regex to flags, credential patterns to credentials, IP:port patterns from scan output to hosts, 'Permission denied'/'Access denied' to failed_attempts
Implement to_context_summary() method on KnowledgeBase that generates a concise text summary for injection into the LLM system prompt
In runner.rs build_system_prompt(), append the KnowledgeBase summary so the LLM always sees current state
Implement deduplication: don't add the same host/credential/flag twice
Implement has_new_findings_for(specialist_name: &str) -> bool method that checks if there are new findings since the last time a specialist ran (this drives the re-activation loop)
Write unit tests for pattern extraction (FLAG regex, credential patterns, IP patterns), deduplication, and has_new_findings_for logic

userStories.title: Implement Specialist Agent trait and orchestrator
userStories.description: As a Redtrail system, I want a Specialist trait that defines domain-expert agents and an Orchestrator that activates specialists based on KnowledgeBase findings so that the right expert is automatically dispatched for each discovered attack surface.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/agent/specialist.rs with Specialist trait: name() -> String, description() -> String, activation_conditions(&KnowledgeBase) -> bool, system_prompt(&KnowledgeBase) -> String, tool_names() -> Vec<String>, max_turns() -> u32
Orchestrator holds: Vec<Box<dyn Specialist>>, shared KnowledgeBase, all registered tools, LLM provider

Orchestrator::run() implements the activation loop: (1) Run Recon specialist first, (2) examine KB, (3) for each specialist where activation_conditions(kb)==true AND (hasn't run yet OR has_new_findings_for(name)), dispatch it, (4) specialist gets its own AgentRunner with filtered tools and domain-specific system prompt, (5) findings merge back into shared KB, (6) repeat 2-5 until no new specialists activate or global turn limit reached, (7) generate report
Orchestrator replaces the current single AgentRunner::run() call in main.rs
Implement create_specialists() function in main.rs (initially empty, specialists added in later stories)
Write unit tests for orchestrator activation loop logic with mock specialists

userStories.title: Implement Recon Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want a Recon Specialist that performs network scanning and service fingerprinting so that it discovers all hosts, ports, and services on the target network as the first step of any engagement.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/specialists/recon.rs implementing the Specialist trait
activation_conditions: always returns true (recon runs first)

system_prompt includes: role (network reconnaissance expert), methodology (ping sweep, port scan, service detection, banner grabbing, DNS enumeration, traffic sniffing), output expectations (populate KnowledgeBase with discovered hosts and services)
System prompt references Shannon's recon.txt patterns for effective enumeration guidance

userStories.title: Implement Web Enumeration Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want a Web Enumeration Specialist that discovers hidden directories, APIs, virtual hosts, and web application structure so that the Web Exploitation specialist has a complete attack surface to work with.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/specialists/web_enum.rs implementing the Specialist trait
activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has discovered_hosts with HTTP/HTTPS services (ports 80, 443, 8080, 8443, or 'http'/'https' in service name)

system_prompt includes: role (web enumeration expert), methodology (directory fuzzing with ffuf/gobuster, virtual host enumeration, robots.txt/sitemap.xml, API endpoint discovery, technology fingerprinting, hidden parameter discovery), common paths to check

userStories.title: Implement Web Exploitation Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want a Web Exploitation Specialist with deep knowledge of web vulnerabilities (SQLi, XSS, SSRF, SSTI, CSRF, command injection, file upload, deserialization, GraphQL) so that it can exploit discovered web applications.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/specialists/web_exploit.rs implementing the Specialist trait
activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has HTTP/HTTPS services with discovered endpoints or web apps

system_prompt includes: role (web exploitation expert), methodology (test each parameter for SQLi, XSS, SSRF, SSTI, command injection; test file uploads for bypass; test JWT tokens for forgery; test GraphQL for introspection; test APIs for IDOR), payload knowledge (common SQLi payloads, XSS vectors, SSTI templates for Jinja2/Twig/Freemarker, SSRF internal targets), WAF bypass techniques
System prompt references Shannon's exploit-injection.txt for SQLi/command injection patterns

userStories.title: Implement Credential Attack Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want a Credential Attack Specialist that performs brute-force attacks, password spraying, and hash cracking against discovered login services so that weak credentials are found and can be used for lateral movement.
acceptanceCriteria: activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has services that accept authentication (SSH, FTP, SMB, HTTP with login forms, MySQL, PostgreSQL, RDP)

system_prompt includes: role (credential attack expert), methodology (try default creds first, then common username/password combos, then targeted wordlists), wordlist locations in toolbox (/usr/share/wordlists/), hash cracking with hashcat/john, credential reuse strategy (try discovered creds on all services)

userStories.title: Implement SMB Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want an SMB Specialist with deep knowledge of SMB/CIFS protocol exploitation so that misconfigured file shares, anonymous access, and sensitive data exposure are discovered and exploited.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/specialists/smb.rs implementing the Specialist trait
activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has hosts with ports 139 or 445 open

system_prompt includes: role (SMB/CIFS protocol expert), methodology (null session enumeration, anonymous share listing, authenticated share access with discovered creds, file download from shares, user enumeration via RID cycling, password policy extraction), common sensitive file patterns to look for
Write unit test for activation_conditions (checks for ports 139/445 in discovered_hosts)

userStories.title: Implement Database Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want a Database Specialist with expertise in MySQL, PostgreSQL, and Redis exploitation so that databases with weak authentication or misconfigurations are enumerated and data is extracted.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/specialists/database.rs implementing the Specialist trait
activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has hosts with ports 3306 (MySQL), 5432 (PostgreSQL), or 6379 (Redis) open

system_prompt includes: role (database exploitation expert), methodology (anonymous/unauthenticated access, try discovered creds, enumerate databases/tables, extract sensitive data, Redis CONFIG exploitation, MySQL UDF for code execution), common data exfiltration queries

userStories.title: Implement Privilege Escalation Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want a Privilege Escalation Specialist with deep Linux privesc knowledge so that when a low-privilege shell is obtained, it systematically escalates to root using SUID, sudo, cron, capabilities, PATH hijacking, and kernel exploits.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/specialists/privesc.rs implementing the Specialist trait
activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has access_levels with privilege_level != 'root' (low-privilege shell obtained)

system_prompt includes: role (Linux privilege escalation expert), methodology (run linpeas, check SUID binaries against GTFOBins, analyze sudo -l output, inspect cron jobs for writable scripts, check capabilities, find writable directories in PATH, check kernel version for known exploits), escalation chains (e.g., cron → writable script → root), common misconfigurations

userStories.title: Implement Pivoting Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want a Pivoting Specialist with expertise in SSH tunneling, chisel, proxychains, and multi-hop networks so that when a host is compromised, internal networks are discovered and accessed through tunnels.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/specialists/pivoting.rs implementing the Specialist trait

activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has access_levels with privilege_level == 'root' AND (host has multiple network interfaces OR new unreachable hosts were discovered from compromised host)

system_prompt includes: role (network pivoting and lateral movement expert), methodology (check ifconfig/ip addr for multiple interfaces, scan internal networks through compromised host, set up SSH dynamic SOCKS proxy, configure proxychains, use chisel for reverse tunnels, multi-hop SSH chains), tunnel management (track active tunnels, clean up on completion)

userStories.title: Implement Active Directory Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want an Active Directory Specialist with deep knowledge of AD attacks (Kerberoasting, AS-REP roasting, DCSync, Golden Ticket, Pass-the-Hash) so that AD domains are fully compromised when detected.
acceptanceCriteria: activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has hosts with port 88 (Kerberos), 389/636 (LDAP), or when domain name/DC is detected in service banners or DNS

system_prompt includes: role (Active Directory attack expert), methodology (LDAP enumeration → find users with SPNs → Kerberoast → crack hashes → check AS-REP roastable accounts → use credentials for lateral movement → DCSync for NTLM hashes → forge Golden Ticket), impacket tool usage patterns, hash cracking strategies, domain trust exploitation

userStories.title: Implement Binary Exploitation Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want a Binary Exploitation Specialist with knowledge of buffer overflows, ROP chains, format strings, and shellcode so that vulnerable binaries on custom TCP services are exploited.
acceptanceCriteria: activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has hosts with non-standard TCP services (not HTTP/SSH/SMB/DNS/DB — custom ports with unknown services or banner patterns suggesting a custom binary)

system_prompt includes: role (binary exploitation expert), methodology (connect to service, analyze behavior, download binary if possible, run checksec, find buffer overflow offset with cyclic patterns, check protections NX/ASLR/canary/PIE, craft exploit with pwntools, techniques: ret2win, shellcode injection, ret2libc, format string leaks, ROP chains), pwntools usage patterns

userStories.title: Implement Container/Cloud Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want a Container/Cloud Specialist with expertise in Docker escapes, Kubernetes attacks, and cloud metadata exploitation so that containerized environments are fully compromised.
acceptanceCriteria: activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has: Docker socket found (/var/run/docker.sock), K8s API detected (ports 6443, 8443, 10250), cloud metadata service reachable (169.254.169.254), or container environment detected (/.dockerenv, cgroup indicators)

system_prompt includes: role (container and cloud security expert), methodology (check if inside container, detect privileged mode, check for docker socket mount, enumerate K8s secrets/pods/services, SSRF to metadata service, image layer analysis with trivy, breakout via host filesystem mount), escape techniques for each vector

userStories.title: Implement Supply Chain Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want a Supply Chain Specialist with knowledge of git history mining, CI/CD injection, and dependency confusion attacks so that source code repositories and build pipelines are exploited.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/specialists/supply_chain.rs implementing the Specialist trait
activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has: Gitea/GitLab/GitHub service detected, CI/CD service detected (Jenkins, Drone, etc.), or package registry detected

system_prompt includes: role (supply chain security expert), methodology (git clone repos, mine git history for secrets with trufflehog/gitleaks, analyze CI/CD configs for injection points, dependency confusion via package registry uploads, build artifact backdooring), git commands for history analysis

userStories.title: Implement AI/LLM Security Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want an AI/LLM Security Specialist with knowledge of prompt injection, RAG poisoning, guardrail bypass, and agent manipulation so that AI-powered applications are exploited.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/specialists/ai_llm.rs implementing the Specialist trait
activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has: endpoints with 'chat', 'llm', 'ai', 'prompt', 'agent' in URL path or response, or JSON APIs that accept 'message'/'prompt' fields

system_prompt includes: role (AI/LLM security expert), methodology (system prompt extraction via role-play injection, confidential data exfiltration via encoding tricks, RAG document poisoning, guardrail bypass techniques, tool/function manipulation in AI agents, indirect prompt injection via stored content), common injection payloads and encoding bypasses (base64, ROT13, character-by-character extraction)

userStories.title: Implement MCP Security Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want an MCP Security Specialist with knowledge of MCP protocol attacks (tool poisoning, rug pull, server impersonation, cross-tool contamination) so that MCP-based AI systems are exploited.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/specialists/mcp_security.rs implementing the Specialist trait
activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has: endpoints with '/mcp/', 'tools', 'registry' in URL path suggesting MCP protocol, or JSON responses with 'tool' definitions

system_prompt includes: role (MCP protocol security expert), methodology (enumerate MCP servers via gateway/registry, inspect tool descriptions for hidden <IMPORTANT> blocks, check for shadow tools in manifest, test rug pull by toggling admin endpoints, register rogue servers to test impersonation, read documents for cross-tool injection payloads, trigger full attack chains via agent chat endpoints), MCP protocol structure knowledge

userStories.title: Implement DNS Specialist agent
userStories.description: As a Redtrail orchestrator, I want a DNS Specialist that attempts zone transfers and subdomain enumeration when DNS services are discovered.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/specialists/dns.rs implementing the Specialist trait
activation_conditions: true when KnowledgeBase has hosts with port 53 open

system_prompt includes: role (DNS security expert), methodology (identify DNS servers, attempt zone transfers AXFR, enumerate subdomains, check for DNS rebinding, reverse DNS lookups for discovered IPs)

userStories.title: Implement network_scan tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want a network_scan tool that wraps nmap with structured JSON output so that I can discover hosts, open ports, and running services on target networks.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/network_scan.rs implementing the Tool trait

Input JSON: { 'target': string (IP/CIDR/range, required), 'mode': string (optional: 'ping_sweep', 'port_scan', 'service_detection', 'full' — default 'service_detection'), 'ports': string (optional, e.g., '1-10000' or '22,80,443,3306') }
Internally runs nmap with -oX flag for XML output, then parses XML into structured JSON

Output JSON: { 'hosts': [{ 'ip': string, 'hostname': string, 'state': string, 'ports': [{ 'port': number, 'protocol': string, 'state': string, 'service': string, 'version': string }] }], 'scan_time': number }
Uses shell_exec internally to run nmap
Add quick-xml crate to Cargo.toml for XML parsing, or parse with regex if simpler
Write unit test with sample nmap XML output to verify parsing

userStories.title: Implement brute_force tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want a brute_force tool that wraps hydra for credential attacks on discovered services.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/brute_force.rs implementing the Tool trait

Input JSON: { 'service': string (required: 'ssh', 'ftp', 'smb', 'http-form', 'mysql', 'rdp', 'telnet'), 'target': string (required: IP or IP:port), 'username': string (optional), 'userlist': string (optional, default '/usr/share/wordlists/common-users.txt'), 'password': string (optional), 'passlist': string (optional, default '/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt'), 'threads': number (optional, default 4) }
Output JSON: { 'found': [{ 'username': string, 'password': string }], 'attempts': number, 'duration_secs': number }
Parses hydra output to extract successful logins
Write unit test with sample hydra output

userStories.title: Implement ssh_exec tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want an ssh_exec tool that executes commands on remote hosts via SSH using discovered credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/ssh_exec.rs implementing the Tool trait

Input JSON: { 'host': string (required), 'port': number (optional, default 22), 'username': string (required), 'password': string (optional), 'key_path': string (optional), 'command': string (required) }
Uses sshpass + ssh for password auth, -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no for lab environments
Write unit test for command construction

userStories.title: Implement file_ops tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want a file_ops tool that can read/write files and transfer between hosts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/file_ops.rs implementing the Tool trait
Input JSON: { 'action': string ('read', 'write', 'transfer'), 'path': string, 'content': string (for write), 'source': string (for transfer), 'destination': string (for transfer) }
Output JSON: { 'content': string (for read), 'success': boolean, 'message': string }

userStories.title: Implement dns_tools tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want a dns_tools tool for DNS queries, zone transfers, and subdomain enumeration.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/dns_tools.rs implementing the Tool trait
Input JSON: { 'action': string ('query', 'zone_transfer', 'reverse_lookup', 'enum_subdomains'), 'domain': string, 'server': string (optional), 'record_type': string (optional, default 'ANY') }
Output JSON: { 'records': [{ 'name': string, 'type': string, 'value': string }], 'zone_transfer_success': boolean }
Write unit test with sample dig output

userStories.title: Implement packet_capture tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want a packet_capture tool for traffic sniffing to capture cleartext credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/packet_capture.rs implementing the Tool trait
Input JSON: { 'interface': string (optional), 'filter': string (optional BPF), 'duration_secs': number (optional, default 10), 'count': number (optional, default 100) }
Auto-scans for credential patterns (FTP USER/PASS, HTTP Basic)

userStories.title: Implement smb_tools tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want an smb_tools tool for SMB share enumeration and file access.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/smb_tools.rs implementing the Tool trait

Input JSON: { 'action': string ('list_shares', 'list_files', 'get_file', 'enum_users'), 'host': string, 'username': string (optional), 'password': string (optional), 'share': string (for file ops), 'path': string (optional) }
Output JSON varies by action: shares list, file list, file content, user list

userStories.title: Implement db_client tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want a db_client tool for MySQL, PostgreSQL, and Redis interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/db_client.rs implementing the Tool trait

Input JSON: { 'type': string ('mysql', 'postgresql', 'redis'), 'host': string, 'port': number (optional), 'username': string (optional), 'password': string (optional), 'query': string (for SQL), 'command': string (for Redis) }
Wraps mysql CLI, psql CLI, redis-cli

userStories.title: Implement tunnel_manager tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want a tunnel_manager tool for SSH tunnels, chisel tunnels, and SOCKS proxies.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/tunnel_manager.rs implementing the Tool trait

Input JSON: { 'action': string ('ssh_local', 'ssh_remote', 'ssh_dynamic', 'chisel_client', 'list', 'stop'), 'host': string, 'username': string, 'password': string (optional), 'local_port': number, 'remote_target': string, 'tunnel_id': string (for stop) }
Output JSON: { 'tunnel_id': string, 'local_port': number, 'status': string, 'tunnels': [object] (for list) }
Tunnels run in background, tracked by PID

userStories.title: Implement privesc_scan tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want a privesc_scan tool that enumerates Linux privilege escalation vectors.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/privesc_scan.rs implementing the Tool trait
Runs: find / -perm -4000, sudo -l, crontab, getcap -r /, PATH analysis

userStories.title: Implement ad_tools tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want an ad_tools tool wrapping impacket and ldapsearch for Active Directory attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/ad_tools.rs implementing the Tool trait

Input JSON: { 'action': string ('enumerate', 'kerberoast', 'asreproast', 'dcsync', 'golden_ticket', 'pth', 'crack_hash'), 'domain': string, 'dc_ip': string, 'username': string (optional), 'password': string (optional), 'hash': string (optional), 'target_user': string (optional), 'hash_file': string (optional), 'wordlist': string (optional) }
Wraps: ldapsearch, impacket-GetUserSPNs, impacket-GetNPUsers, impacket-secretsdump, impacket-ticketer, hashcat/john

userStories.title: Implement binary_tools tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want a binary_tools tool for binary analysis and exploitation with pwntools, ropper, and checksec.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/binary_tools.rs implementing the Tool trait

Input JSON: { 'action': string ('checksec', 'find_gadgets', 'create_payload', 'run_exploit', 'analyze'), 'binary_path': string (optional), 'target_host': string (optional), 'target_port': number (optional), 'exploit_script': string (optional: pwntools Python code), 'offset': number (optional), 'target_addr': string (optional) }
run_exploit writes script to /tmp and executes with python3

userStories.title: Implement container_tools tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want a container_tools tool for Docker and Kubernetes attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/container_tools.rs implementing the Tool trait

Input JSON: { 'action': string ('docker_inspect', 'docker_exec', 'docker_escape_check', 'k8s_get_secrets', 'k8s_get_pods', 'k8s_get_services', 'image_inspect', 'check_socket'), 'container': string (optional), 'namespace': string (optional), 'command': string (optional) }
docker_escape_check detects: privileged mode, docker socket mount, host PID/network, writable host FS

userStories.title: Implement python_exec tool
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want a python_exec tool that executes Python scripts and one-liners inside the toolbox.
acceptanceCriteria: Create redtrail/src/tools/python_exec.rs implementing the Tool trait
Exactly one of 'code' or 'script_path' must be provided
For inline code: writes to /tmp/probe_<uuid>.py and executes
Lists available libraries in description: pwntools, requests, impacket, scapy, beautifulsoup4, paramiko

userStories.title: Implement flag detection and auto-reporting
userStories.description: As a Redtrail agent, I want automatic flag detection that scans all tool output for FLAG{...} patterns and records them so that no flag is missed.
acceptanceCriteria: Scans every tool output for regex FLAG\{[^}]+\}
When detected: add to KnowledgeBase.flags (deduplicated), auto-create Finding, log to console
Integrate into tool dispatch loop in runner.rs
Also scan LLM responses for flags
Add flag count to report summary
Write unit tests for regex matching

userStories.title: Extend HTML report for specialist-based engagements
userStories.description: As a user, I want the HTML report to show which specialists ran, their findings, the attack path graph, discovered credentials, and all captured flags.
acceptanceCriteria: Add specialists section: which ran, what they found
Add attack path visualization (text-based step-by-step chain)
Keep backward compatibility with web-only reports

userStories.title: Integration test: web-only backward compatibility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify that existing web-only scanning (SQLi, XSS, IDOR) still works after all v2 changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Write integration test with --target http://... against mock server
Verify HttpClient, Crawler, SqliTester, XssTester, IdorTester still work
Verify system prompt guides web scanning when only --target is provided

userStories.title: Integration test: specialist activation loop
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test that verifies the orchestrator correctly activates specialists based on KnowledgeBase findings.
acceptanceCriteria: Write integration test with mock specialists and mock KnowledgeBase
Verify: Web specialist activates when HTTP ports found
Verify: SMB specialist activates when port 445 found
Verify: Privesc activates when low-privilege access gained
Verify: specialists don't re-activate without new findings
Verify: new findings from one specialist can trigger another
Verify: KnowledgeBase is shared across all specialists
